quiz.title: Pre-calculus: Unit 12 Quiz 11
quiz.subtitle: 12.11: Conic Sections (Calculations)
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Parabola properties and equations, applications of parabolas, ellipse properties and equations, applications of ellipses, hyperbola properties and equations, applications of hyperbolas, circle equations and properties, identifying conics from general form, nonlinear systems with conics, and rotated conics introduction

questions.question: Find the focus of the parabola $(x - 2)^2 = 12(y + 1)$.
questions.explanation: Vertex is $(2, -1)$. $4p = 12 \implies p = 3$. Since it opens upward, focus is at $(2, -1 + 3) = (2, 2)$.

questions.question: A satellite dish has a parabolic cross-section. If the receiver is placed 4 feet from the vertex, what is the equation of the parabola if it opens upward with vertex at $(0,0)$?
Find the vertices of the ellipse $\frac{(x-3)^2}{25} + \frac{(y+1)^2}{9} = 1$.
questions.explanation: Center at $(3, -1)$. $a^2 = 25 \implies a = 5$ (horizontal major axis). Vertices: $(3 \pm 5, -1) = (8, -1)$ and $(-2, -1)$.

questions.question: An elliptical arch has a span of 30 feet and a height of 10 feet. If the center is at $(0,0)$ and the major axis is horizontal, find the value of $b$ (the minor axis length).
questions.explanation: $2a = 30 \implies a = 15$. Height is the semi-minor axis, so $b = 10$.

questions.question: Find the foci of the hyperbola $\frac{y^2}{16} - \frac{x^2}{9} = 1$.
questions.explanation: Center at $(0,0)$. $a^2 = 16 \implies a = 4$, $b^2 = 9 \implies b = 3$. $c^2 = a^2 + b^2 = 25 \implies c = 5$. Vertical transverse axis, foci at $(0, \pm 5)$.

questions.question: Two radio stations are located at the foci of a hyperbola. If the distance between them is 10 miles, what is the value of $c$?
questions.explanation: The foci are at $(\pm c, 0)$. Distance between foci is $2c = 10 \implies c = 5$.

questions.question: Find the center and radius of the circle $x^2 + y^2 + 6x - 8y = 0$.
Identify the conic: $4x^2 + 9y^2 - 8x + 18y - 23 = 0$.
questions.explanation: $A = 4$, $C = 9$. Both positive and $A \neq C$, so it is an ellipse.

questions.question: Use the discriminant to identify the conic: $3x^2 + 2xy + 3y^2 - 5x + 7y - 2 = 0$.
questions.explanation: $A = 3$, $B = 2$, $C = 3$. Discriminant = $B^2 - 4AC = 4 - 36 = -32$. Since $-32 < 0$, it is an ellipse.

scoreMessages.70: Good work. Review ellipse/hyperbola relationships and completing the square.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing â€” focus on identifying conics from general form and solving nonlinear systems.
scoreMessages.0: Review the standard equations for circles, parabolas, ellipses, and hyperbolas.
